The manufacturer requires dispensing information for clozapine patients. All members of the clozapine treatment team must be entered as users on the local system and must be given the key, PSOLOCKCLOZ. All pharmacists who have the ability to override the lockouts in this option must also hold the key. These pharmacists should be identified by the pharmacy service representative of the clozapine treatment team.

The following step must be taken before an order for clozapine can be entered. If this information is missing, clozapine orders cannot be entered.

Any physician writing an order for clozapine must have a DEA number or VA number entered in the NEW PERSON file. These can be added through the *Add New Providers* option on the *SUPERVISOR’S MENU PSJU FILE*. This must be done before the order is entered. The DEA or VA number cannot be entered during the new order entry process. With the release of YS\*5.01\*90, providers must also hold the YSCL AUTHORIZED security key.

When an order is placed, the system checks for the provider’s DEA number or VA number first. CPRS has implemented an expiration field for the Provider DEA number. If the active DEA license expires, the Provider will be prevented from ordering clozapine until the Provider DEA# is reactivated. Licenses are active for 2 years. There is no message that indicates the DEA number has expired. The user may need to check this file. If the provider is not authorized, the pharmacist will receive the applicable message:

Provider must have a DEA# or VA# to write prescriptions for this drug.

--OR--

Provider must hold YSCL AUTHORIZED key to write prescriptions for clozapine

### FDA Guidelines for Lab Results / Monitoring

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

Enhanced and new functionality includes updated FDA guidelines for using ANC lab results exclusively for treating and monitoring patients taking clozapine, as shown in the following table. The new guidelines replace previous requirements, which included both white blood cell (WBC) count and ANC lab results.

The Mental Health software’s code replaces the WBC/ANC results combination and supports ANC values solely as the lab value of choice.

> **NOTE:** FDA REMS prescribed dosage and monitoring is based on the ANC value only; however, VA requires a matching WBC, indicating the WBC is from the same draw date/time as the ANC.

Table 1: FDA ANC Levels and Monitoring Guidelines

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 23%" />
<col style="width: 21%" />
<col style="width: 55%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr class="header">
<th><strong>ANC Level</strong></th>
<th><strong>ANC Monitoring</strong></th>
<th><strong>Frequency of ANC lab tests</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td>Normal range</td>
<td>ANC ≥ 1500 cmm</td>
<td><p>Weekly (W) for patients 1 – 6 months on therapy</p>
<p>Bi-weekly (B) for patients 6 – 12 months on therapy</p>
<p>Monthly (M) for patients &gt;12 months on therapy</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td>Mild neutropenia</td>
<td>1000 – 1499 cmm</td>
<td>ANC labs 3 times weekly until ANC stabilizes to 1500 cmm or greater</td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td>Moderate neutropenia</td>
<td>500 – 999 cmm</td>
<td>ANC labs Daily until ANC stabilizes to 1000 cmm or greater, then 3 times weekly until ANC stabilizes to 1500 cmm or greater</td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td>Severe neutropenia</td>
<td>&lt; 500 cmm</td>
<td>ANC labs Daily until ANC stabilizes to 1000 cmm or greater, then 3 times weekly until ANC stabilizes to 1500 cmm or greater</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

1.  The ANC unit of measure is expressed as cells per cubic millimeter (cmm) which is equivalent to cu mm, mm<sup>3</sup> or µL.

## Hierarchy for Addressing Clozapine Patient Override Conditions

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The system determines which condition to address first when more than one override condition is presented to the provider and the pharmacist:

1.  If the patient is not actively registered, the system will first require that the patient have an active registration number, which is either temporary or permanent. Patient registration must be addressed first in *all* scenarios; if the patient is not actively registered, the system will not return lab results. In the event of an emergency, the [Emergancy Registration Override—Overview](#_Emergency_Registration_Override) addresses assigning a temporary local authorization number.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/175.png)An Emergency registration requires that the patient have a Normal ANC of 1500 cmm or greater in the last 7 days with a matching WBC. Otherwise, NCCC will need to be contacted to assign a permanent number before any other overrides can be processed.

2.  All clozapine treatment is based on the presence of an ANC result in the last 7 days with a matching WBC – if there are no ANC results, this condition must be addressed next before any other condition and requires an override. See [No ANC Results – Overview](#no-anc-results-overview)
3.  The next condition that must be addressed is if there is an ANC result in the last 7 days but no matching WBC. For example, if the system detects mild or moderate to severe neutropenia (ANC is present but no matching WBC is found), the system will require the No Matching WBC condition process flow be followed. An override is required. See [No Matching WBC (ANC present) - Overview](#no-matching-wbc-anc-present-overview).

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/176.png)A Matching WBC result is collected at the same draw date/time as the ANC.

The following table illustrates the combinations of conditions and which must be addressed first.

Hierarchy for Addressing Clozapine Patients

| Condition            | ANC Present? | Matching WBC? | Action                                                                                      |
|--------------------------|------------------|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| \*No active registration | Yes              | Yes               | Address no active registration. REQUIRES Normal ANC result in last 7 days with matching WBC |
| Normal ANC result        | Yes              | Yes               | No override needed                                                                              |
| Normal ANC result        | Yes              | No                | Address ‘No WBC’                                                                                |
| Mild Neutropenia         | Yes              | Yes               | Address ‘Mild neutropenia’                                                                      |
| Mild Neutropenia         | Yes              | No                | Address ‘No WBC’                                                                                |
| Moderate to Severe       | Yes              | Yes               | Address ‘Moderate/Severe neutropenia’                                                           |
| Moderate to Severe       | Yes              | No                | Address ‘No WBC’                                                                                |
| No ANC                   | No               | No/Yes            | Address ‘No ANC’                                                                                |

\*This condition must be addressed first in *all* situations. If there is no registration, no labs can be displayed.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/177.png)

Two override conditions cannot be combined into a single flow. There cannot be two override reason codes in one session.

The following highlighted sections illustrate new features for treating/managing patients taking clozapine.

## Clozapine Patient Authorization 

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

Patients treated with clozapine must be enrolled in the clozapine treatment program at the NCCC and receive a valid authorization number with a status of Active. If the patient’s status changes from Active to Discontinued, a lockout will prevent being able to order clozapine.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/178.png)There are only two applicable statuses for a patient: Active (A) or Discontinued (D). Previously Pre-Treatment (P) and On-Hold (H) statuses were used but are now obsolete.

The option to override the condition when a patient is not actively registered is described in [Emergency Registration Override – Overview](#_Ref460249304). A temporary local authorization number is assigned in the event the patient has never been registered in the local VistA system or has been registered and is now on discontinued status. A patient that is discontinued due to inactivity in the program must be registered again and receive a new Clozapine Registration ID.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr class="header">
<th><strong>Inpatient enrollment with NCCC</strong></th>
<th><p>Patients are registered at the local facility where they are being treated.</p>
<p>NCCC assigns a valid Authorization number indicating the patient status as ‘Active’.</p>
<p>See <a href="#clozapine-patient-authorization">Clozapine Patient Authorization</a>.</p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>28 day trigger</strong></td>
<td>If the patient is new to the program, the NCCC Patient Authorization status automatically changes to Discontinued if there is no active clozapine order in the first 28 days.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>56 day trigger</strong></td>
<td>If the patient is currently Active and an inpatient order or outpatient prescription has been activated, but there have been no new active clozapine prescriptions or orders within the past 56 days, the NCCC Patient Authorization status is automatically changed to Discontinued.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Temporary authorization number expiration trigger</strong></td>
<td>If the patient has been assigned a temporary local authorization number, it will expire after the 4-day active period and if the NCCC has not yet issued a permanent Clozapine Authorization Number, the status automatically changes to Discontinued.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

2.  All status changes are automatic based on triggers. Pharmacists are not permitted to make manual changes from Active to Discontinued status.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr class="header">
<th><strong>Nightly data transmission</strong></th>
<th><p>The VistA system automatically notifies the NCCC of all status changes in the past 24 hours in the nightly data transmission and includes: site ID, patient name, last 4 of SSN, and trigger.</p>
<p>See <a href="#vista-daily-clozapine-transmission">VistA Daily Clozapine Transmission</a> description.</p></th>
</tr>
</thead>

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/179.png)CPRS has implemented an expiration field for the provider DEA#. If the active DEA license expires, the provider will be prevented from ordering clozapine until the provider DEA# is reactivated. Licenses are active for 2 years. There is no message that indicates the DEA# has expired. The user may need to check the File 200/New Person File.

## Authorized Pharmacist with PSOLOCKCLOZ key 

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

Any clozapine order that requires either a National or Local Override requires the pharmacist to have the PSOLOCKCLOZ key. For clozapine orders, the only time a pharmacist is not required to have the PSOLOCKCLOZ key is when the ANC results are Normal within the last 7 days (equal to or greater than 1500 cmm) with a matching WBC.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 49%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>When is PSOLOCKCLOZ key required?</strong></td>
<td><p>The following conditions require the primary pharmacist and an Approving member of the clozapine team to both have the PSOLOCKCLOZ key:</p>
<ul>
<li><blockquote>
<p><strong>Mild neutropenia</strong> – Local Override</p>
</blockquote></li>
<li><blockquote>
<p><strong>Moderate to Severe</strong> <strong>neutropenia</strong> – National Override</p>
</blockquote></li>
<li><blockquote>
<p><strong>No ANC Results</strong> – National Override or Special Conditions Local Override</p>
</blockquote></li>
<li><blockquote>
<p><strong>ANC with No Matching WBC</strong> – National Override</p>
</blockquote></li>
<li><blockquote>
<p><strong>Unregistered Patient</strong> – Emergency Registration Local Override with written order</p>
</blockquote></li>
</ul></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Verify pharmacist is authorized with PSOLOCKCLOZ key</strong></td>
<td><p>When the pharmacist, who is processing the Pending or Written Order that includes an override, does not hold the PSOLOCKCLOZ key, this message displays:</p>
<p>You Are Not Authorized to Override! See Clozapine Manager with PSOLOCKCLOZ key.</p>
<p>Pending Orders return to pending status and written orders issue a hard stop and the order is deleted and not processed.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/180.png)The PSOLOCKCLOZ key is required for overrides in Inpatient Medications serving as the security key for all clozapine overrides. There is no separate <u>PSJ</u>LOCKCLOZ key.

## Approving Member with PSOLOCKCLOZ key

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The authorized pharmacist is required to select an Approving Member of the clozapine team when an override is required. The Approving Member must have an active PSOLOCKCLOZ key but does not need to be a pharmacist. The primary pharmacist will select an Approving Member from a list before the order can be dispensed.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/181.png)The Approving Member may or may not be aware their name was selected as an Approving Member. An auto-notification is sent via VistA Alerts when the override order is dispensed to inform them. Refer to [Auto-Notify Provider and Approving Member](#auto-notify-provider-and-approving-member).

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 49%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Approving Member of the clozapine team</strong></td>
<td><p>During processing of the order, the primary pharmacist will be prompted with the following message:</p>
<p>Approving member of the clozapine team</p>
<p>The pharmacist is required to select from a list of approved members with the PSOLOCKCLOZ key by entering a partial name or entering “??”.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/182.png)When searching for an Approving Member, two ‘??’ must be entered. If only one ‘?’ is entered, the system returns information that is not related and is out of the control of the pharmacy application.

## Auto-Notify Provider and Approving Member

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

When a clozapine order is verified, an auto-notification that the override order is completed is sent to the Provider and the Approving Member via VistA Alerts. The VistA Alert displays in both GUI and VistA Backdoor Pharmacy.

The alert is a non-actionable information alert. When the Provider or Approving Member clicks on the alert, it is automatically deleted from the view.

The VistA Alert message includes:

- Patient name \[concatenated\] and last 4 of SSN
- Alert Date/Time
- Message: CLOZAPINE OVERRIDE RX PROCESSED with the Alert Date/Time attached at the end

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 49%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr class="header">
<th><strong>Auto-notification via VistA Alerts</strong></th>
<th><p>An auto-notification is sent when any National or Local Override is required to process the clozapine order.</p>
<ol start="3">
<li><blockquote>
<p>It is possible that an Approving Member of the clozapine team may not be aware that their name was used for the override.</p>
</blockquote></li>
</ol>
<p>The alert is viewable in both GUI and VistA Backdoor Pharmacy.</p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Non-actionable alert</strong></td>
<td>Once the alert is viewed, the user clicks on the alert and it is deleted from the view. No other action is needed.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Sample GUI VistA Alert</strong></td>
<td>The GUI VistA Alert is accessed through CPRS. A sample screen is shown in the figure below.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/183.png)

## Edit Order (ED) – Safety Checks for New Order

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

### Editable fields in Pharmacy

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

During pharmacy processing, several fields are editable. Those fields are delineated by an asterisk (\*). When fields with an \* are edited, the system will discontinue the current order and create a new order.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/184.png)New clozapine orders that are created through Edit Order will perform order and safety checks when the new order is created.

> ![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/185.png)

: Sample Screen of Editable Fields (\*)

### Default Stop Dates

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

#### Default Stop Date Management for Doses of Clozapine

When a patient is on a management dose of clozapine, the system defaults the stop date for the following:

- 7 days default for a patient on weekly dispense frequency (W) can be edited to less than the default 7 days
- 14 days default for a patient on bi-weekly dispense frequency (B) can be edited to less than the default 14 days
- 28 days default for a patient on monthly dispense frequency (M) can be edited to less than the default 28 days

#### Default Stop Date for Emergency 4-day Supply of Clozapine (Inpatient)

- When an emergency 4-day supply is dispensed to the patient, the system defaults the stop date to 4 days. The limit of 4 days cannot be exceeded; however, clozapine can be dispensed for less than 4 days.

## Copy Order (CO) – Safety Checks for New Order

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The pharmacist may use the Copy Order option to copy patient and medication information to create a new clozapine order. This has no effect on the original order. The copy function can be from an active, discontinued, or expired order.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/186.png)New clozapine orders that are created through Copy Order will perform order and safety checks when the new order is created.

| Copy Order (CO) access in VistA Backdoor | The pharmacist may locate this hidden menu by entering two ‘??’. |
|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|

> ![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/187.png)

Sample Screen of Copy Order

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/188.png)For complex orders, Copy Order is not typically used by the pharmacist. However, if Copy Order is used for a complex order, the pharmacist can only copy the child orders.

### Renew Order – Blocked in IP

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The Renew order option (RN) is blocked and is not functional in Inpatient Medications. In addition, the provider is unable to renew an order in CPRS.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 49%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Inpatient Medications Renew Order (RN) blocked in VistA Backdoor</strong></td>
<td><p>The Renew (RN) function displays on the menu to the IP pharmacist.</p>
<p>If the IP pharmacist enters RN to renew an order, the following messages displays:</p>
<p>Clozapine orders cannot be renewed</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## Override Reason Codes

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The system generates override reason codes that are presented to the pharmacist when processing an order that requires an override to dispense clozapine. Prior override reason codes have been retired and are no longer applicable, except \#7 NCCC AUTHORIZED.

| Code | Short Description                  | Long Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|----------|----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 7        | NCCC AUTHORIZED                        | NCCC Authorized National Override in effect allowing the prescription to proceed without valid, safe (normal or mild neutropenia) blood test results. (Added in 2006 software update).                                                           |
| 8        | REGISTER NON-DUTY HR/WEEKEND (MAX4DAY) | Emergency Registration Local Override allows patient without a local clozapine registration to continue clozapine treatment during non-duty hours/weekends (not to exceed 4 days) until the provider can register him on the next NCCC duty day. |
| 9        | PRESCRIBER APPROVED 4 DAY SUPPLY       | Special Conditions Local Override to dispense a 4-day supply to a patient without ANC lab results in the last 7 days.                                                                                                                            |
| 10       | MILD NEUTROPENIA PRESCRIBER APPROVED   | Mild Neutropenia Local Override for patients with ANC 1000 - 1499 cmm.                                                                                                                                                                           |

### Request for Override of Pharmacy Lockout Form

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

Whenever a National Override is required for clozapine, the Request for Override of Pharmacy Lockout form must be completed and the hard copy sent to NCCC before the NCCC can approve the National Override. This form gives the pertinent information to the NCCC for them to determine if the National Override should be approved. See the following [Request For Override Pharmacy Lockout Form](#Request_For_Override_Form):

<span id="Request_For_Override_Form" class="anchor"></span>![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/189.png)

## Total Daily Dose 

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The Total Daily dose (mg/day) identifies the currently prescribed total daily dose for a patient across all active Outpatient prescriptions and Inpatient orders. It is not intended as a reflection of a patient’s doses taken or administered.

Like Outpatient Pharmacy, which prompts the pharmacist to manually calculate the total daily dose in mg/day across all active orders and enter the total when a clozapine prescription is entered, the Inpatient Medications pharmacy likewise includes this functionality with the same prompt when a clozapine order is entered.

3.  Order transactions that will issue this prompt to the IP pharmacist include: New Order (NO), Copy Order (CO), and Edit order (ED). \*Renew order (RN) is not available.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 49%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr class="header">
<th><strong>Pharmacist prompted to manually calculate and enter total daily dose</strong></th>
<th><p>When there is a new inpatient order for clozapine, the pharmacist is prompted to manually calculate and enter the required total daily dose (mg/day) at this prompt:</p>
<p>CLOZAPINE dosage (mg/day) ? :</p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
</tbody>
</table>

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/190.png)

Calculations may be simple or include levels of complexity. The mg/day calculation is based on Active prescriptions and orders across IP and OP at the time the manual calculation is requested.

Listed below are sample scenarios for calculating Total Daily Dose (mg/day):

\#1 Inpatient Medications - Patient is admitted to hospital as an inpatient

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr class="header">
<th><p>When a new order for an inpatient is entered, the pharmacist will calculate the prescribed active order(s). This scenario has only active IP prescriptions.</p>
<p>Calculating the Total Daily Dose:</p>
<p>25 mg AM dose – Active IP order      </p>
<p><u>50 mg PM dose</u> – Active IP order</p>
<p><strong>75 mg Total Daily Dose</strong></p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
</tbody>
</table>

\#2 Inpatient Medication dose changes during the day

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr class="header">
<th><p>When the prescribed IP dose changes during the day, the current order is Discontinued and a new order is processed and becomes the Active order.</p>
<p>Calculating the Total Daily Dose:</p>
<p>   25 mg AM dose – Active IP order - <em>Discontinued</em></p>
<p>50 mg AM dose – Changed 25 mg to 50 mg</p>
<p><u>75 mg PM dose</u> – New Active IP order/dose</p>
<p><strong>   125 mg Total Daily Dose</strong></p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
</tbody>
</table>

\#3 Inpatient orders put on “Hold” when patient goes on leave as an Outpatient

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 100%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr class="header">
<th><p>When an inpatient goes on authorized leave – all current inpatient meds are put on “hold” status, but the patient is still considered an inpatient in the VistA system. With the “hold” status, the med is still “active” but the VistA BCMA blocks a nurse from administering an inpatient dose while the patient is on leave (pass).</p>
<p>The patient will not be consuming the inpatient dose while on pass, but only the outpatient dose. The pharmacist will not add both the inpatient and outpatient doses into this calculation for Total Daily Dose. </p>
<p>Calculating the Total Daily Dose:</p>
<p>    50 mg AM dose – Active “hold” IP order – not taken while on leave</p>
<p>50 mg AM dose – Active OP order</p>
<p>75 mg PM dose – Active “hold” IP order – not taken while on leave</p>
<p><u>75 mg PM dose</u> – Active OP order</p>
<p><strong>125 mg Total Daily Dose</strong></p></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
</tbody>
</table>

### Complex Clozapine Orders

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

A complex order must be ordered by the provider in CPRS which will create a “complex” Pending Order (parent order) to be processed by the pharmacist. The system manages the complex order by using the parent order which is broken out into child orders for each dose/dispense instruction.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/191.png)If the clozapine order is not received as a Pending Order, e.g., a written order is given to the pharmacist, the system treats this as two (or more) different orders and does not organize them as a complex order.

Complex clozapine orders consist of one or more conjunctions of “and” or “then” or both. For example, a parent order may be 50 mg QD (am) *and* 100 mg QHS (pm). The figure below illustrates how the parent order is then broken out into Child Order \#1 and Child Order \#2.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/192.png)

Sample Complex Parent Order

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/193.png)It has been noted that complex orders – regardless of whether they are clozapine or not – have issues with the start and stop dates calculating correctly. For clozapine orders, an interim solution will be a warning message to alert the pharmacist that the stop date of the child orders may be incorrect. New code will correctly calculate the stop date and display it to the pharmacist in a warning message. If the final child order stop date exceeds the date noted in the warning message, the pharmacist can edit the stop date to match what is in the warning message. This issue will be fixed in a future version of CPRS.

The Inpatient Medication pharmacist will process the Pending Complex order which will start with Child Order \#1 then move to Child Order \#2 and so on. The warning message displays immediately after the parent complex order is selected from the Pending Complex list, as illustrated in the figure below.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/194.png)

Warning Message for Complex Orders

If the order is noted by the pharmacist to exceed the parameters of the current patient dispense frequency, e.g., more than 7 days for a patient on a 7-day frequency, it is recommended that the pharmacist cancel the order and contact the provider to reenter the order correctly.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/195.png)The pharmacist needs to make note of the calculated stop date in the warning message so that the final child order stop date in the complex order group can be edited if it exceeds the stop date noted in the warning message.

The calculated stop date is based on the CPRS order date + the patient’s current dispense frequency:

- If the patient is on a weekly (W) dispense frequency, the stop date cannot exceed 7 days from the CPRS order date.
- If the patient is on a bi-weekly (B) dispense frequency, the stop date cannot exceed 14 days from the CPRS order date.
- If the patient is on a monthly (M) dispense frequency, the stop date cannot exceed 28 days from the CPRS order date.

#### Calculating Total Daily Dose in Complex Orders

The pharmacist is prompted to calculate and enter the Total Daily Dose for each child order. Complex orders consisting of the ‘and’ conjunction within the complex orders (e.g., 50 mg in the morning and 100 mg at bedtime) where the child orders are on the same day, then both child orders would have a Total Daily Dose of 150 mg.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/196.png)While this may not be optimum, NCCC will review the Total Daily Doses before sending to the FDA.

When the complex order consists of ‘then’ conjunctions (e.g., 50 mg QD x 2 *then* 100 mg QD), each of the orders are on different days and the Total Daily Dose would be accurately entered.

When the complex order uses a combination of ‘and’ and ‘then’ conjunctions, the pharmacist will calculate the Total Daily Dose by adding the child orders that are applicable. For example, if the patient is on a Weekly (W) frequency (7 days), the following table illustrates how the Total Daily Dose is calculated:

Calculating Total Daily Dose for Complex Orders

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 13%" />
<col style="width: 14%" />
<col style="width: 14%" />
<col style="width: 14%" />
<col style="width: 42%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr class="header">
<th><strong>Start Date</strong></th>
<th><strong>Child Order #1</strong></th>
<th><strong>Child Order #2</strong></th>
<th><strong>Child Order #3</strong></th>
<th><strong>Total Daily Dose</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td>11/08/16</td>
<td>50 mg QD x 2 days</td>
<td><p><em><strong>and</strong></em> 100 mg QHS (pm)</p>
<p>for 7 days total</p></td>
<td></td>
<td><p>Add up the orders with <em><strong>and</strong></em> conjunction for the same day:</p>
<p>50 mg + 100 mg = <strong>150 mg</strong>. Calculate/enter this for Child #1 and Child #2 Total Daily Dose.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td>11/10/16</td>
<td></td>
<td>100 mg QHS for 7 days total</td>
<td><em><strong>then</strong></em> 100 mg QD (for 5 days)</td>
<td><p>Add up the orders for Child #2 and Child #3:</p>
<p>100 mg QHS + 100 mg QD = <strong>200 mg</strong></p>
<p>Calculate/enter this for Child #3 for Total Daily Dose</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

#### Auto-Verification of Orders

If the pharmacist account is set to auto-verify orders, when each child order is Accepted (AC), it is also verified at that moment.

#### Stop Processing Child Orders

During processing of each child order and where an override is required, the pharmacist is prompted to override the order by selecting ‘Yes’ or ‘No.’ If the user exits out (‘^’) at any time before the last child order is accepted, all child orders are returned to Pending status<u>.</u>

#### Complex Clozapine Orders with National Override in Effect

In the event a complex clozapine order is received and a National Override is in effect and no associated lab date within the past 7 days, the warning message illustrated previously will not display (the stop date for complex clozapine orders is based on the CPRS order date + patient current dispense frequency).

## Normal ANC – Overview

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The FDA defines Normal – sometimes referred to as “safe” or “passing” ANC results – as equal to or greater than 1500 cmm. When the system identifies that the ANC results are Normal and a matching WBC is present, the provider completes the order which is sent as a Pending Order to pharmacy.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/197.png)If a matching WBC result is not present, the system will alert the provider with a message that requires a National Override. See [No Matching WBC (ANC present) – Overview](#no-matching-wbc-anc-present-overview).

### Normal ANC – Pharmacy 

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

When ANC results are normal, the pharmacist will verify the order and dispense the clozapine.

\*This flow assumes the patient is actively registered and the provider is authorized.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 49%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td><p><strong>Normal ANC –</strong></p>
<p><strong>Results display</strong></p></td>
<td><blockquote>
<p>When the pharmacist receives a Pending or written order and the ANC results are normal with a matching WBC, the system displays the following:</p>
<p>* Most recent WBC and NEUTROPHILS ABSOLUTE (ANC) results *</p>
<p>     performed on MonthXX,XXXX are:</p>
<p>     WBC: XXXX</p>
</blockquote>
<p>     ANC: XXXX</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist does not require PSOLOCKCLOZ key</strong></td>
<td>The pharmacist is not required to have the PSOLOCKCLOZ key to process a clozapine order when the ANC results are normal.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## Mild Neutropenia – Overview

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The FDA defines Mild neutropenia as an ANC result from 1000 to 1499 cmm. New ANC lab test monitoring guidelines for Mild neutropenia are:

Test ANC labs 3x weekly until levels stabilize to greater than or equal to 1500 cmm.

With this condition, a Local Override is required. In the event a matching WBC result is not present with the ANC, the system will alert the provider with a message that will require a National Override. See [No Matching WBC (ANC present) – Overview](#no-matching-wbc-anc-present-overview)<u>.</u>

## Mild Neutropenia – Pharmacy 

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

When ANC results indicate Mild neutropenia (ANC is from 1000-1499 cmm), either a Pending or written order is received. Mild Neutropenia requires a Local Override.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/198.png)New guidelines are to test ANC labs 3x weekly until levels stabilize to greater than or equal to 1500 cmm. Both the provider and pharmacist are alerted.

\*This flow assumes the patient is actively registered and the provider is authorized.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 49%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist not authorized – Pending or written order</strong></td>
<td><p>If the pharmacist is not authorized with the PSOLOCKCLOZ key, this message displays:</p>
<p>You Are Not Authorized to Override! See Clozapine Manager with PSOLOCKCLOZ key.</p>
<p>For a Pending Order, a hard stop is issued and the order is sent back to Pending status. For a written order, the system issues a hard stop and the order is deleted and not processed.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Lab results returned</strong></td>
<td><p>Lab results are returned to the pharmacist in the following message:</p>
<p>Now doing clozapine Order checks. Please wait...</p>
<p>* Last xx WBC (EFF. mm-dd-yyyy) and ANC results were: *</p>
<p>      WBC ANC</p>
<blockquote>
<p>mm/dd/yyyy@hh:mm Results: xxxx xxxx</p>
<p>mm/dd/yyyy@hh:mm Results: xxxx xxxx</p>
</blockquote>
<p>&lt;<em>Displays <strong>up to last four results</strong> in the last 30 days&gt;</em></p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Mild neutropenia override prompt</strong></td>
<td><p>After the labs are displayed, the pharmacist is prompted to override the abnormal ANC results with this message:</p>
<p>Override reason: MILD NEUTROPENIA PRESCRIBER APPROVED</p>
<p>Test ANC labs 3x weekly until levels stabilize to greater than or equal to 1500.</p>
<p>Do you want to override and issue this order? Y/N.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist does not want to override</strong></td>
<td><p>In the event the pharmacist selects ‘No’ and does not want to override the order:</p>
<p>For a Pending Order, a hard stop is issued and the order is sent back to Pending status. For a written order, the system issues a hard stop and the order is deleted and not processed.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist prompted to enter Approving Member</strong></td>
<td><p>When ‘Yes’ is entered, the pharmacist is prompted to enter the name of an Approving Member of the clozapine team. The list of authorized users with the PSOLOCKCLOZ key is retrieved by entering a partial name or entering ‘??’</p>
<p>Approving member</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist enters Remarks/Special Instructions</strong></td>
<td><p>The pharmacist is required to enter free text in the Remarks/Special Instructions field with a limit of 200 characters.</p>
<p>Remarks/Special Instructions</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Dispense clozapine</strong></td>
<td>The pharmacist is prompted to verify the override order and dispense the clozapine.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Auto-notification to Provider and Approving Member</strong></td>
<td>When the clozapine override order is complete, the system sends an auto notification to the provider and Approving Member via VistA Alerts. See <a href="#auto-notify-provider-and-approving-member">Auto-Notify Provider and Approving Member.</a></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Store and transmit patient data</strong></td>
<td><p>Patient data is stored in the long-term temporary global and transmitted to the National Clozapine data files nightly, including the override reason code:</p>
<p>(10) MILD NEUTROPENIA PRESCRIBER APPROVED</p>
<p>See <a href="#vista-daily-clozapine-transmission">VistA Daily Clozapine Transmission</a>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## Moderate to Severe Neutropenia – Overview

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

FDA defines Moderate neutropenia as ANC 500-999 cmm and severe neutropenia as ANC less than 500 cmm. Both Moderate and Severe neutropenia require the same lab test monitoring guidelines as they are both less than 1000 cmm:

Test ANC labs daily until levels stabilize to equal to or greater than 1000 cmm, thenTest ANC labs 3x weekly until levels stabilize to greater than or equal to 1500 cmm.

When there is Moderate to Severe neutropenia with a matching WBC result present, a National Override is required. If a matching WBC result is not present, the system will alert the provider that the system will address the ‘No Matching WBC’ issue first. See [No Matching WBC (ANC present) – Overview](#no-matching-wbc-anc-present-overview)<u>.</u>

### Moderate to Severe Neutropenia – Pharmacy (National Override expired)

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

When a National Override is approved by the NCCC and expires before the pharmacist processes the Pending or written order, the order is no longer valid.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/199.png)Once a National Override is in effect for *any* order, the pharmacist must complete the order before midnight of the day it was approved by NCCC, or the approval expires.

\*This flow assumes the patient is actively registered and the provider is authorized.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 49%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>National Override approval expired at midnight of day it was approved</strong></td>
<td><p>If the NCCC approval expires before the clozapine order is processed, the following message displays to the pharmacist:</p>
<p>National Override expired at midnight on &lt;<em>Jun 29, 2016</em>&gt;</p>
<p>* Last xx WBC and ANC results were: *</p>
<p>      WBC ANC</p>
<blockquote>
<p>mm/dd/yyyy@hh:mm Results: xxxx xxxx</p>
<p>mm/dd/yyyy@hh:mm Results: xxxx xxxx</p>
</blockquote>
<p>&lt;<em>Displays <strong>up to last four results</strong> in the last 30 days&gt;</em></p>
<blockquote>
<p>Permission to dispense clozapine has been denied. If the results of the latest Lab Test drawn in the past 7 days show ANC below 1000/mm3 and you wish to dispense outside the FDA and VA protocol ANC limits, document your request to Request for Override of Pharmacy Lockout (from VHA Handbook 1160.02) Director of the VA National Clozapine Coordinating Center (Phone: 214-857-0068 Fax: 214-857-0339) for a one-time override permission.</p>
<p>A CBC/Differential including ANC Must Be Ordered and Monitored on a Daily basis until the ANC above 1000/mm3 with no signs of infection. If ANC is between 1000-1499, therapy can be continued but physician must order lab test three times weekly.</p>
</blockquote>
<p>Also make sure that the LAB test, ANC is set up correctly in the Mental Health package using the CLOZAPINE MULTI TEST LINK option.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Hard stop</strong></td>
<td><p>If a National Override is not in effect, the system issues a hard stop.</p>
<ol start="4">
<li><blockquote>
<p>When the NCCC issues a National Override approval, the date is recorded in the local VistA system indicating approval until midnight of that date.</p>
</blockquote></li>
</ol></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### Moderate to Severe – Pharmacy (National Override in Effect)

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

When a National Override is in effect and the local VistA system date updated in File \#603.01, the pharmacist can proceed with processing the Pending or written order.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/200.png)Both Moderate and Severe neutropenia have the same REMS treatment protocol since both have ANC results less than 1000 cmm.

\*This flow assumes the patient is actively registered and the provider is authorized.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/201.png)Once the ANC levels stabilize to greater than or equal to 1000 cmm, the testing continues at 3x weekly until the ANC stabilizes to equal to or greater than 1500 cmm.

## No ANC Results – Overview

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The system relies on the presence of an ANC result in order to treat patients receiving clozapine, with exceptions as noted to override in certain conditions. The system requires an ANC within the last 7 days. See Hierarchy for Addressing Clozapine Patient Override Conditions for treating clozapine patients based on lab results.

For registered patients with a history of normal ANC results, the requirement for a normal ANC result in the last 7 days may be bypassed in order to meet an urgent need under special conditions.

In CPRS, when the provider is notified that there are no ANC results in the last seven days, they will make a decision based on the following:

1.  Non-emergency

> If this is not an emergency, the provider will request a National Override to dispense the clozapine at the patient’s normal frequency. When the National Override is authorized and recorded in the local VistA system, the provider reenters the order and a Pending order to dispense clozapine at the patient’s normal frequency is sent.

> For example, this may be used when:

1.  The patient’s last blood test was done at another facility and isn’t in the local VistA system but the provider has seen the results
2.  The provider feels it is unnecessary – perhaps the patient is at end of life or some other medical condition – and there is no need to keep drawing blood
2.  Emergency 4-day supply

> In an emergency where a 4-day supply is needed, the provider may choose to use a Special Conditions Local Override which optionally allows a one-time 4-day emergency supply to be dispensed for specific prescriber-approved reasons.

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/202.png)A written prescription or order is *required*. Special Conditions Local Override is not supported in CPRS – no Pending Order is available.

> If the patient is an Inpatient, the prescriber-approved reason will be:

1.  IP Order Override with Outside Lab Results

### No ANC – Non-emergency Pending Order 

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

Scenario 1 – When there is a Pending Order from CPRS or the Inpatient Medications Pharmacist receives a written order, it indicates the provider has requested a National Override to dispense clozapine at the patient’s normal frequency. This is for a non-emergency condition only. A National Override must be in effect.

## No ANC Special Conditions for Emergency – Overview

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The Special Conditions Local Override is for emergency conditions when there are No ANC results in the last 7 days and a one-time 4-day emergency supply is needed. A history of normal ANC values is required. A written order from the provider is the only way to use the Special Conditions Local Override in the VistA Backdoor Pharmacy.

5.  When a one-time 4-day supply is dispensed, the system will not block the provider or pharmacist from using the Special Conditions override more than once; however, notification to NCCC is provided when an emergency supply is dispensed to discourage abuse of using this option except in cases of emergency.

> Inpatient Special Conditions

> When the patient is an inpatient, the provider who wishes to dispense an emergency supply will be required to document the following reason in the written order for the pharmacist:

> \(4\) IP Order Override with Outside Lab Results

6.  In VistA Backdoor Pharmacy, the pharmacist is required to record the ANC results from another facility, including date/time, in the Remarks/Special Instructions field. VACO has indicated that documentation of the ANC lab result from an outside system is mandatory. NCCC requires a provider/prescriber signed document which *should* be scanned into the medical record but is always placed in the patient file.

Provider Decision

The FDA REMS supports the clinical medical decisions of the provider to determine when to prescribe clozapine – even if the ANC is null. Providers often feel that subjecting a clozapine patient to more blood testing in excess of what is required by the FDA regulation is usually due to administrative barriers and is considered unnecessary. The option to have a Special Conditions Local Override allows the provider to make that decision and also meet documentation requirements.

If the Special Conditions event should happen during the week, the NCCC requires written documentation of the ANC and Date of Testing before they will input codes for a National Override. VACO has mandated that NCCC have a written record of all override requests submitted by the facility and that the ANC is recorded on that request. See [No ANC – Emergency IP Special Conditions](#no-anc-emergency-ip-special-conditions)

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

\*This flow assumes the patient is actively registered and the provider is authorized.

7.  In this scenario, a National Override is NOT in effect because this situation requires a written order from the provider to the Inpatient Medications Pharmacist, as well as a Local Override.

## No Matching WBC (ANC present) – Overview 

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

When there is an ANC result in the last 7 days but no matching WBC, regardless of whether it is a normal result or indicates mild or moderate to severe neutropenia, the system will address the ‘No Matching WBC’ condition first. A warning message to the provider will require a National Override in order to dispense clozapine. Remember that the system can only address one condition at a time following this hierarchy. See Hierarchy for Addressing Clozapine Patient Override Conditions to determine which condition is addressed first.

12. A Matching WBC result is collected at the same draw date/time as the ANC.

</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Auto-notification to Provider and Approving Member</strong></td>
<td>When the clozapine override order is complete, the system sends an auto notification to the provider and Approving Member via VistA Alerts. See <a href="#auto-notify-provider-and-approving-member">Auto-Notify Provider and Approving Member</a>.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Store and transmit patient data</strong></td>
<td><p>Patient data is stored in the long-term temporary global and transmitted to the National Clozapine data files nightly, including the override reason code:</p>
<p>#7 – NCCC AUTHORIZED</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### No Matching WBC – Pharmacy (No National Override)

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The pharmacist will be able to complete an order when there is No Matching WBC with the ANC *only* when a National Override is in effect.

\*This flow assumes the patient is actively registered and the provider is authorized.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 49%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>No Matching WBC –<br />
No National Override in Effect</strong></td>
<td><p>In the event there is No National Override in effect with No Matching WBC, this is the message that displays before the hard stop:</p>
<p>Now doing clozapine Order checks. Please wait...</p>
<blockquote>
<p>&lt;<em>Displays up to the last 4 results within the last 30 days, if available</em>&gt;</p>
</blockquote>
<p>Permission to dispense clozapine has been denied. The result of the latest Lab Test drawn in the past 7 days shows ANC results but No Matching WBC. If you wish to dispense outside the FDA and VA protocol ANC limits, document your request to Request for Override of Pharmacy Lockout (from VHA Handbook 1160.02) Director of the VA National Clozapine Coordinating Center (Phone: 214-857-0068 Fax: 214-857-0339) for a one-time override permission.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>No Matching WBC – Must request a National Override to proceed<br />
</strong></td>
<td><p>In order to proceed when a National Override is not in effect and there is No Matching WBC, an approval from NCCC must be requested.</p>
<p>Once NCCC gives approval, the pharmacist can continue processing the order.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## Emergency Registration Override – Overview

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

When an authorized provider enters an order in CPRS and the patient is not actively registered, the provider is notified to either request a permanent number be assigned by the NCCC (non-emergency) or a temporary local authorization number be assigned through the VistA Backdoor Pharmacy (emergency).

When there is an urgent need to dispense clozapine before the NCCC can assign a permanent number, to avoid interrupting therapy, the provider can authorize a limited supply to be dispensed – not to exceed 4 days – with a temporary local authorization number.

13. In order to use the Emergency Registration Override, the patient must have a Normal ANC result (equal to or greater than 1500 cmm) with a matching WBC in the last 7 days.

### Valid reasons for an Emergency Registration Override:

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

An Emergency Registration Override is typically warranted for the following reasons:

1.  The patient has a current NCCC registration at another VistA facility
2.  Inpatient transferred from another facility
3.  Outpatient from another facility becomes an inpatient
4.  The patient has never been registered at the local facility
5.  The patient status has changed from Active to Discontinued
6.  An Outpatient with a prescription that had previously been filled outside of the VA or at another VA facility arrives during NCCC non-duty hours
14. A Clozapine Authorization Number is only valid for a patient at the facility where they were registered. When a Clozapine patient arrives at a new facility, they need to be re-registered with a new Clozapine Authorization Number before the facility’s CPRS and Pharmacy applications will allow a Clozapine order to be processed.

### Emergency Registration Override – VistA Backdoor Pharmacy 

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

Using VistA Backdoor Pharmacy, the Inpatient pharmacist must receive a written order from the provider to assign a temporary local authorization number when the patient is not actively registered and there is an urgent need to dispense up to a 4-day supply.

15. CPRS currently does not support a Pending Order for an Emergency Registration Override. It must be processed as a written order.

### Temporary number

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

A unique sequential seven character temporary tag is assigned, for example: ‘Z442001’, where:

Temporary tag = Z + 3 digit facility code = XXX (for example, 442) + 3 digit unique number = 001-999

In the event all the 999 numbers linked to the temporary tag ‘Z’ are used, the system will move up the alphabet to ‘Y’ and assign the next set of numbers 001-999 with a ‘Y’ temporary tag, and so on. Only upper case letters are supported.

### Pharmacist Flow

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

The pharmacist has received a written clozapine order from the provider to assign a temporary local authorization number:

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 49%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Patient not actively registered</strong></td>
<td><p>When the patient is not actively registered, the system displays this message:</p>
<p>* This patient has no clozapine registration number *</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist not authorized</strong></td>
<td><p>If the pharmacist is not authorized with the PSOLOCKCLOZ key, the system displays this message:</p>
<p>You Are Not Authorized to Override! See Clozapine Manager with PSOLOCKCLOZ key.</p>
<p>The system issues a hard stop and the written order is deleted and not processed</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Prompt to register patient</strong></td>
<td><p>The authorized pharmacist is prompted to register the patient with a temporary local authorization number:</p>
<p>Do you want to register this patient with a temporary local authorization number in the Clozapine registry? Y/N</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist does not want to override</strong></td>
<td>In the event the pharmacist selects ‘No’ and does not want to register the patient, the order is deleted and not processed.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Prompt to enter Provider</strong></td>
<td><p>The system prompts the pharmacist to enter the name of the provider.</p>
<p>Provider Responsible</p>
<p>The pharmacist will continue to be prompted until an authorized provider name is entered or the pharmacist enters ^ to stop the order.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Provider not authorized</strong></td>
<td><p>If the selected provider is not authorized to prescribe clozapine, this message displays to the pharmacist:</p>
<p>Provider must have a DEA# or VA# to write prescription for clozapine.</p>
<p>--OR--</p>
<p>Provider must hold YSCL AUTHORIZED key to write prescriptions for clozapine.</p>
<ol start="16">
<li><blockquote>
<p>Remember to check the license expiration date if the provider is not authorized.</p>
</blockquote></li>
</ol></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Assign temporary local authorization number</strong></td>
<td><p>The authorized pharmacist is prompted to register the patient with a temporary local authorization number in the Clozapine registry.</p>
<p>This message displays to the pharmacist:</p>
<p>Would you like to override the registration requirement and assign a temporary local authorization number for &lt;LASTNAME,FIRSTNAME&gt; &lt;last 4 digits of SSN&gt; &lt;Z224001&gt;? Y/N</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Pharmacist does not want to override</strong></td>
<td>In the event the pharmacist selects ‘No’ and does not want to override the registration requirement, the order is deleted and not processed.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Error message if conditions not met</strong></td>
<td><p>In the event any of the following are true:<br />
(1) the ANC result in the last 7 days is less than 1500 cmm, (2) there is no ANC result, or (3) the ANC does not have a matching WBC, the system displays this message and the order is deleted and not processed:</p>
<p>Emergency Registration Local Override for non-registered clozapine patients requires ANC levels greater than or equal to 1500.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Results display if ANC Normal and prompt to override</strong></td>
<td><p>When the pharmacist selects ‘Yes’ to override, the new temporary local authorization number is accepted. This message displays when the ANC is 1500 cmm or greater:</p>
<blockquote>
<p>* Most recent WBC and NEUTROPHILS ABSOLUTE (ANC) results *</p>
<p>     performed on are: MonthXX,XXXX</p>
<p>     WBC: XXXX</p>
</blockquote>
<p>     ANC: XXXX</p>
<p>Override reason: REGISTER NON-DUTY HR/WEEKEND (MAX 4DAY)</p>
<p>Do you want to override and issue this order? Y/N</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Pharmacist does not want to override</strong></td>
<td>In the event the pharmacist selects ‘No’ and does not want to override the order, it is deleted and not processed.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Pharmacist prompted to enter Approving Member</strong></td>
<td><p>The pharmacist is prompted to enter the name of an Approving Member of the clozapine team. The list of authorized users with the PSOLOCKCLOZ key is retrieved by entering a partial name or entering ‘??’</p>
<p>Approving member</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Pharmacist enters Remarks/Special Instructions</strong></td>
<td><p>The pharmacist is required to enter free text in the Remarks/Special Instructions field with a limit of 200 characters.</p>
<p>Remarks/Special Instructions</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Real-time notification of temporary local authorization number to NCCC MailMan Group</strong></td>
<td><p>Once the order is complete and dispensed, the system sends a <strong>real-time</strong> message to the NCCC MailMan Group which contains this information:</p>
<p>Current date/time</p>
<p>Add</p>
<p>From</p>
<p>Temporary local authorization number (e.g., Z442001)</p>
<p>Station #.</p>
<p>Social Security Number</p>
<p>Dispense frequency (W)</p>
<ol start="17">
<li><blockquote>
<p>The only valid response for dispense frequency is W (Weekly)</p>
</blockquote></li>
</ol></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Auto-notification to Provider and Approving Member</strong></td>
<td>When the clozapine order is complete, the system sends an auto notification to the provider and Approving Member via VistA Alerts that the order has been dispensed. See <a href="#auto-notify-provider-and-approving-member">Auto-Notify Provider and Approving Member</a>.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Store and transmit patient data</strong></td>
<td><p>Patient data is stored in the long-term temporary global and transmitted to the National Clozapine data files nightly, including the override reason code:</p>
<p>(8) REGISTER NON-DUTY HR/WEEKEND (MAX4DAY)</p>
<p>See <a href="#vista-daily-clozapine-transmission">VistA Daily Clozapine Transmission</a>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The temporary local authorization number is valid for 4 days. If the pharmacist chooses to create a number but does not finish the order process, either because of choosing not to complete the order or lack of an ANC of 1500 cmm or greater, the pharmacist may restart the order with the already-created temporary local authorization number until that number expires at midnight of the 4<sup>th</sup> day.

## Other Scenarios

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

Additional potential scenarios may exist. The following can be adapted for other potential lab conditions besides Mild neutropenia.

### National Override in Effect – Takes Precedence

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

Scenario 1 – The Provider sends a Pending Order for mild neutropenia based on lab results at the time of the order. However, sometime during the day, a National Override was authorized for this patient, which is still in effect until midnight of the day it was authorized. In this scenario, if the system sees that a National Override is in effect, even though the labs indicate Mild neutropenia, the reason code returned to the pharmacist will be NCCC AUTHORIZED.

\*This flow assumes the patient is actively registered and the provider is authorized.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 49%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pending Order – Mild Neutropenia</strong></td>
<td><p>The authorized pharmacist reviews the Pending Order. If the system detects a National Override is in effect, even though the lab results indicate Mild neutropenia, which does not require a National Override, the system will return the reason code to the pharmacist: NCCC AUTHORIZED rather than MILD NEUTROPENIA PRESCRIBER APPROVED<u>.</u></p>
<ol start="18">
<li><p>When a National Override is in effect, it takes precedence over any other condition.</p></li>
</ol></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist not authorized</strong></td>
<td><p>If the pharmacist is not authorized with the PSOLOCKCLOZ key, this message is displayed:</p>
<p>You Are Not Authorized to Override! See Clozapine Manager with PSOLOCKCLOZ key.</p>
<p>A Pending Order is returned to pending status, while a written order issues a hard stop and the order is deleted and not processed.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Lab results returned</strong></td>
<td><p>Lab results are returned to the pharmacist in the following message:</p>
<p>Now doing clozapine Order checks. Please wait... &lt;<em>Displays up to last four results in the last 30 days&gt;</em></p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Override prompt</strong></td>
<td><p>After the labs are displayed, the pharmacist is prompted to override the abnormal ANC results with this message:</p>
<p>Override reason: NCCC AUTHORIZED</p>
<p>Do you want to override and issue this order? Y/N.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist does not want to override</strong></td>
<td><p>In the event the pharmacist selects ‘No’ and does not want to override the order:</p>
<p>For a Pending Order, a hard stop is issued and the order is sent back to Pending status. For a written order, the system issues a hard stop and the order is deleted and not processed.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist prompted to enter Approving Member</strong></td>
<td><p>When ‘Yes’ is entered, the pharmacist is prompted to enter the name of an Approving Member of the clozapine team. The list of authorized users with the PSOLOCKCLOZ key is retrieved by entering a partial name or entering ‘??’</p>
<p>Approving member</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist enters Remarks/Special Instructions</strong></td>
<td><p>The pharmacist is required to enter free text in the Remarks/Special Instructions field with a limit of 200 characters.</p>
<p>Remarks/Special Instructions</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Dispense clozapine</strong></td>
<td>The pharmacist is prompted to verify the override order and dispense the clozapine.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Auto-notification to Provider and Approving Member</strong></td>
<td>When the clozapine override order is complete, the system sends an auto notification to the provider and Approving Member via VistA Alerts. See <a href="#auto-notify-provider-and-approving-member">Auto-Notify Provider and Approving Member</a>.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Store and transmit patient data</strong></td>
<td><p>Patient data is stored in the long-term temporary global and transmitted to the National Clozapine data files nightly, including the override reason code:</p>
<p>(7) NCCC AUTHORIZED</p>
<p>See <a href="#vista-daily-clozapine-transmission">VistA Daily Clozapine Transmission</a>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### Managing a Complex Clozapine Order

<!-- back-to-toc -->[↑ Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)

Scenario 2 – Complex clozapine orders are only applicable in the Inpatient Medication pharmacy and must be received as a Pending Order *from the provider* originating in CPRS. Complex orders have more than one dose/dispense set of instructions and are separated by conjunctions (“and”, “then”, or a combination of both). See [Complex Clozapine Orders](#complex-clozapine-orders).

\*This flow assumes the patient is actively registered and the provider is authorized.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 49%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">

</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist not authorized</strong></td>

<p>You Are Not Authorized to Override! See Clozapine Manager with PSOLOCKCLOZ key.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Select Child Order #1 from PENDING COMPLEX list – New Order (NO)</strong></td>

<tr class="even">
<td><strong>c</strong></td>

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/204.png)

|                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|--------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Warning Message to Inpatient Medication pharmacist | Immediately upon selection of the parent order, the following warning message in the following figure displays to alert the pharmacist to review and make note of the calculated stop date. |

![](inpatient-medications-pharmacist-s-user-manual-psj-5-423/205.png)

19. If any of the child orders (prior to the final child order) exceed the warning message stop date, the entire complex order is cancelled and the prescriber is contacted to reenter the order correctly.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 49%" />
</colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Review each child order, including the stop date</strong></td>
<td><p>The pharmacist reviews each child order, making note of the stop date in Field 5. If the stop date in any child order exceeds the calculated stop date in the warning message, the pharmacist will <strong>cancel the order</strong> and contact the provider to reenter the order with the correct stop date.</p>
<p>Assuming all the other child orders in the complex do not exceed the stop date, but the <strong>final child order exceeds the warning message stop date in Field 5</strong>, the pharmacist would then edit the stop date of the final child order to match what is in the warning message.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td><strong>Pharmacist finishes Child Order #1 –<br />
Finish (FN)</strong></td>
<td>If the stop date (Field 5) for Child Order #1 does not exceed the stop date in the warning message, the pharmacist selects Finish (FN) to finish the first child order.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>If the stop date for Child Order #1 exceeds the calculated stop date</strong></td>

</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Pharmacist accepts Child Order #1 –<br />
Accept (AC)</strong></td>
<td>The pharmacist Accepts (AC) Child Order #1 and continues processing the order.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Calculate Total Daily Dose for Child<br />
Order #1</strong></td>
<td>The pharmacist is prompted to calculate and enter the required Total Daily Dose. See Total Daily Dose for complex child orders.</td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr class="even">
<td><strong>Labs returned and Order Checks started</strong></td>
<td><p>Lab results are returned to the pharmacist in the following message:</p>
<p>Now doing clozapine Order checks. Please wait... &lt;<em>Displays up to last four results in the last 30 days&gt;</em></p></td>
</tr>
<tr class="odd">
<td></td>
<td></td>

20. Depending on the underlying ANC result, the system will display the appropriate screens to the pharmacist for processing the order. In this example, the patient has Mild Neutropenia and the screens appropriate to Mild Neutropenia will be displayed.
